name: Decagon
description: Decagon is presented as an approach for modeling polypharmacy side effects. It constructs a multimodal graph incorporating protein-protein interactions, drug-protein target interactions, and polypharmacy side effects (represented as distinct edge types of drug-drug interactions). The model develops a new graph convolutional neural network designed for multirelational link prediction within these multimodal networks, enabling it to handle a large number of edge types. Unlike simpler approaches, Decagon can predict the exact clinical side effect manifested by a given drug combination. It accurately predicts polypharmacy side effects, outperforming baselines by up to 69%, and demonstrates capabilities in automatically learning representations indicative of co-occurring side effects while effectively sharing model parameters across different edge types for both molecularly based and non-molecular side effects.
